name: mettle-syntax
description: Syntax highlighting, folding, bracket matching, and linting for Mettle (.mettle) — a typed language that compiles to x86-64 assembly. See README for links to the language docs.

mettle.linter.compilerEnabled.description: Run the Mettle compiler on save for semantic diagnostics (types, scope, undefined symbols).
mettle.linter.compilerEnabled.markdownDescription:
When enabled, the extension runs the `mettle` compiler **on save** and shows real compiler errors and warnings in the Problems panel. Regex-based checks still run while you type.

Requires a `mettle` binary (workspace `bin/` or `PATH`).

mettle.linter.compilerPath.description: Path to the mettle executable. Empty: auto-detect workspace bin/, then PATH.
mettle.linter.compilerPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the Mettle executable. Can be relative to the workspace folder or absolute.

If empty, the extension looks for `bin/mettle` or `bin/mettle.exe` (and a few fallbacks) under the workspace, then uses `mettle` / `mettle.exe` on your `PATH`.

See the repo `docs/compilation.md` for how to build or install the compiler.

mettle.linter.stdlibPath.description: Optional --stdlib directory override for the linter invocation.
mettle.linter.stdlibPath.markdownDescription:
If set, passed to the compiler as `--stdlib <dir>` when linting. Use when you maintain a custom standard library layout.

Leave empty to use the compiler’s default bundled stdlib resolution (see `docs/compilation.md`).
